itinerary:
Here is a moderate-paced 3-day itinerary for a New York trip focused on museums, food, history, and outdoors using real places from the search:

Day 1: Historic Downtown & Museums
- Morning: Visit Fraunces Tavern Museum to explore Revolutionary War history [frauncestavernmuseum.org]
- Late Morning: Walk around South Street Seaport Historic District for a blend of historic views and outdoor atmosphere.
- Lunch: Dine at Pedro's for an authentic local eatery experience [pedrosbrooklyn.com]
- Afternoon: Visit the National Museum of the American Indian to learn about native cultures [americanindian.si.edu]
- Early Evening: Relax at Pumphouse Park, an outdoor green space nearby.

Day 2: Museum & Cultural Exploration in Lower East Side
- Morning: Explore the Lower East Side Tenement Museum for immigrant history and stories [tenement.org]
- Lunch: Eat at The Musket Room, known for refined and creative dishes [themusketroom.com]
- Afternoon: Visit Storefront for Art and Architecture for modern artistic inspiration [storefrontnews.org]
- Coffee Break: Have a coffee at Think Coffee to recharge [thinkcoffee.com]
- Evening: Casual dining or drinks at Freemans, a historic stomach-friendly spot [freemansrestaurant.com]

Day 3: Outdoor and Art Walks with Eating
- Morning: Visit Chatham Square and then stroll through Little Flower Playground for some outdoor urban vibe.
- Lunch: Try Nha Trang One for Vietnamese cuisine close by [nhatrangnyc.net]
- Afternoon: Explore local artworks including The Sphere and Netherland Monument.
- Late Afternoon Snack: Grab a quick bite or coffee at Starbucks near Battery Park.
- Evening: Relax at nearby parks or explore local food spots based on your preference.

This itinerary balances museum visits, historic sites, enjoyable outdoor parks, and food breaks at interesting local spots, all at a moderate pace to savor the New York experience. Would you like detailed timing suggestions or transport options?

pois.name: National Museum of the American Indian
